sentence_en: She comes from a close family with three brothers and two sisters.
translation_zh: 她来自一个有三个兄弟和两个姐妹的亲密家庭。
notes: Simple sentence. Target span is singular family. Collocations: close family / comes from a family. No culturally sensitive content.

sentence_en: Starting a family is one of the most important decisions in a person's life.
translation_zh: 组建家庭是一个人一生中最重要的决定之一。
notes: Collocation: start a family. Future-planning context natural for Speaking Part 2. No sensitive content.

sentence_en: Her parents moved to a new city before she was born.
translation_zh: 她的父母在她出生之前搬到了一个新城市。
notes: Common plural usage. Simple past tense. Support vocabulary: move / city / born.

sentence_en: Being a good parent requires both patience and a lot of time.
translation_zh: 做一个好父母既需要耐心，也需要花很多时间。
notes: Gerund subject (Being a good parent). Collocations: good parent / requires patience. B1-safe vocabulary throughout.

sentence_en: Young people today have very different values from their parents' generation.
translation_zh: 今天的年轻人与父母那一代的价值观大相径庭。
notes: Cross-topic reuse of value (pf_017). Collocations: parents' generation / younger generation. Common IELTS essay phrase.

sentence_en: Family traditions are often passed down from one generation to the next.
translation_zh: 家庭传统往往从一代人传到下一代。
notes: Collocation: from one generation to the next. Cross-topic reuse of tradition (pf_006). Highly essay-ready phrase.

sentence_en: Their marriage has lasted for more than forty years.
translation_zh: 他们的婚姻持续了四十多年。
notes: Simple declarative. Collocation: a marriage lasts. Numbers and time expressions are L10-12 topic but assumed as prior knowledge.

sentence_en: Many young people delay marriage until they feel financially secure.
translation_zh: 许多年轻人推迟结婚，直到他们感到经济上有保障。
notes: Collocation: delay marriage / financially secure. Common IELTS Speaking Part 3 topic. Cross-topic reuse of financially/secure as support vocabulary.

sentence_en: A strong relationship between parents and children is important for family life.
translation_zh: 父母与子女之间牢固的关系对家庭生活非常重要。
notes: Collocation: strong relationship / relationship between A and B. Cross-topic reuse of family (pf_001) and parent (pf_002).

sentence_en: She has always had a close relationship with her grandmother.
translation_zh: 她与祖母一直关系亲密。
notes: Collocation: close relationship. Simple declarative with adverb always for emphasis. Grandmother is A1 support word.

sentence_en: Eating dinner together as a family is a tradition in many cultures.
translation_zh: 全家人一起吃晚饭是许多文化中的一种传统。
notes: Gerund subject. Cross-topic reuse of culture (pf_016) and family (pf_001). Concrete and culturally neutral example.

sentence_en: The tradition of telling stories to children before bed is still common today.
translation_zh: 睡前给孩子讲故事的传统在今天仍然很普遍。
notes: Collocation: maintain a tradition / still common. Cultural but universally relatable. No sensitive content.

sentence_en: The role of fathers in childcare has changed greatly over the past fifty years.
translation_zh: 过去五十年里，父亲在育儿中的角色发生了很大变化。
notes: Collocation: play a role / the role of. Common IELTS Task 2 sentence structure: The role of X has changed. Cross-topic reuse of father (support word).

sentence_en: Parents play an important role in helping children develop good habits.
translation_zh: 父母在帮助孩子养成良好习惯方面发挥着重要作用。
notes: Fixed collocation: play an important role in. Very common IELTS essay phrase. Cross-topic reuse of parent (pf_002).

sentence_en: Looking after elderly parents is a responsibility that many adult children take seriously.
translation_zh: 照顾年迈的父母是许多成年子女认真对待的责任。
notes: Cross-topic reuse of elderly (pf_009). Collocation: take on a responsibility / feel a sense of responsibility. Common IELTS ageing topic.

sentence_en: Teaching children about responsibility helps them succeed in life.
translation_zh: 教导孩子懂得责任感有助于他们在生活中取得成功。
notes: Gerund subject. Collocation: sense of responsibility. Cross-topic reuse of children (support word).

sentence_en: Many families in China feel it is their duty to care for their elderly relatives at home.
translation_zh: 中国许多家庭认为在家中照顾年迈亲属是自己的责任。
notes: Contains cultural reference to China. Review to ensure it is framed neutrally and does not imply judgment. Cross-topic reuse of responsibility/duty (pf_008).

sentence_en: Elderly people often feel lonely when they live far from their family.
translation_zh: 当老年人离家人很远时，他们往往会感到孤独。
notes: Adjective use of elderly. Collocations: elderly people / feel lonely. Cross-topic reuse of family (pf_001). No sensitive content.

sentence_en: As children grow older
translation_zh: they naturally become more independent.
target_span: 随着孩子长大，他们自然地变得更加独立。
notes: F
column14: Collocation: become independent. Common developmental theme. Cross-topic reuse of child (support word).

sentence_en: She wanted to be financially independent before starting a family.
translation_zh: 她希望在组建家庭之前在经济上独立。
notes: Collocation: financially independent. Cross-topic reuse of family (pf_001). Common life-planning topic in Speaking Part 2.

sentence_en: Parents should support their children's dreams even when they are different from their own.
translation_zh: 父母应该支持孩子的梦想，即使这些梦想与自己的不同。
notes: Verb form taught here. Collocation: support someone's dreams. Cross-topic reuse of parent (pf_002). Modal should for advice tone.

sentence_en: Many people rely on their family for emotional support during difficult times.
translation_zh: 许多人在困难时期依靠家人获得情感支持。
notes: Phrase emotional support uses support as noun not verb. Decide whether to use this as example for noun sense (support_n) or change the sentence to use verb. Flag for review.

sentence_en: She was raised in a small town and later moved to the city for work.
translation_zh: 她在一个小镇上长大，后来为了工作搬到了城市。
notes: Past passive (was raised). Target span: raised. Collocation: be raised in a place. Common in Speaking Part 2 background introductions.

sentence_en: It takes a lot of effort to raise children in a big city.
translation_zh: 在大城市里抚养孩子需要付出很多努力。
notes: Infinitive construction: it takes effort to raise. Cross-topic reuse of city (support word). Teaches active verb form.

sentence_en: Parents have the greatest influence on a child's values and behavior.
translation_zh: 父母对孩子的价值观和行为影响最大。
notes: Collocation: have an influence on. Cross-topic reuse of value (pf_017) and behavior (pf_019). Common IELTS essay sentence.

sentence_en: The culture you grow up in has a strong influence on the way you see the world.
translation_zh: 你所成长的文化对你如何看待世界有着深远的影响。
notes: Collocation: strong influence on. Cross-topic reuse of culture (pf_016). Relative clause structure typical of B1+ writing.

sentence_en: The local community organized activities to help elderly residents feel less isolated.
translation_zh: 当地社区组织了活动，以帮助年迈的居民减少孤独感。
notes: Cross-topic reuse of elderly (pf_009). Collocation: local community / sense of community. Describes community-support function.

sentence_en: Children who grow up in a close community often develop strong social skills.
translation_zh: 在紧密社区中成长的孩子往往能培养出较强的社交能力。
notes: Collocation: close community. Cross-topic reuse of children (support word) and social (support word). Relative clause structure.

sentence_en: Every individual has the right to make their own choices in life.
translation_zh: 每个人都有权在生活中做出自己的选择。
notes: Noun use of individual. Collocation: every individual / each individual. Note: their used as gender-neutral singular pronoun (modern American English standard).

sentence_en: Some cultures place more value on the group than on the individual.
translation_zh: 一些文化更看重群体而非个人。
notes: Group vs individual contrast. Cross-topic reuse of culture (pf_016) and value (pf_017). Classic IELTS Task 2 argument structure.

sentence_en: Learning about another culture helps people develop respect and understanding.
translation_zh: 了解另一种文化有助于人们培养尊重和理解。
notes: Gerund subject. Collocation: learn about a culture / cultural understanding. Positive framing appropriate for IELTS context.

sentence_en: Family values differ greatly from one culture to another.
translation_zh: 家庭价值观因文化不同而存在很大差异。
notes: Collocation: from one culture to another. Cross-topic reuse of value (pf_017) and family (pf_001). Common comparative structure in Task 2 essays.

sentence_en: Respect for older people is a core value in many Asian families.
translation_zh: 尊重老年人是许多亚洲家庭的核心价值观。
notes: Contains regional reference (Asian families). Review to ensure neutral framing. Collocation: core value / shared value. Cross-topic reuse of elderly/older people (pf_009).

sentence_en: Children often adopt the values of their parents without realizing it.
translation_zh: 孩子们往往在不知不觉中接受父母的价值观。
notes: Plural values (target span). Collocation: adopt values / shared values. Cross-topic reuse of parent (pf_002). Natural and neutral example.

sentence_en: A positive attitude toward studying helps children succeed at school.
translation_zh: 积极的学习态度有助于孩子在学校取得成功。
notes: Collocation: positive attitude toward. Note: toward (American) not towards (British). Cross-topic reuse with education.

sentence_en: Her attitude toward family responsibilities changed after she became a parent herself.
translation_zh: 成为父母之后，她对家庭责任的态度发生了变化。
notes: Collocation: attitude toward something / change your attitude. Cross-topic reuse of responsibility (pf_008) and parent (pf_002).

sentence_en: Children often copy the behavior of the adults around them.
translation_zh: 孩子们往往会模仿身边成年人的行为。
notes: American spelling: behavior. Collocation: copy behavior / model behavior. Concrete and relatable. Cross-topic reuse of children (support word).

sentence_en: Good behavior at home often leads to good behavior at school.
translation_zh: 在家里的良好行为通常也会带来在学校的良好表现。
notes: Repetition of behavior emphasizes the cross-context transfer idea. Collocation: good/bad behavior. Simple and clear sentence structure.

sentence_en: Family conflict can have a negative effect on children's mental health.
translation_zh: 家庭冲突会对孩子的心理健康产生负面影响。
notes: Collocation: family conflict / resolve a conflict. Cross-topic reuse of children (support word). Common IELTS Task 2 argument.

sentence_en: Learning to resolve conflict peacefully is an important life skill.
translation_zh: 学会和平解决冲突是一项重要的人生技能。
notes: Collocation: resolve a conflict. Gerund subject. Positive framing appropriate for IELTS. Cross-topic with society and psychology.
